title: sterculia chapelieri
intro: Sterculia chapelieri, also known as the Balsa Tree, is a medicinal plant native to Southeast Asia. It is valued for its bark, which contains tannins and other compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in traditional medicine. The medicinal properties of Sterculia chapelieri have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various skin conditions. The bark can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for medicinal purposes.
